summary: Fifth Third Bancorp is reportedly ahead of schedule in realizing the benefits from its acquisition of Comerica, the largest in the bank's 168-year history. The article suggests that the bank is making significant progress toward achieving deposit growth and cost savings as anticipated from the $10.9 billion deal. Further details regarding these new deposits and cost savings are expected to be elaborated in the full article.

title: Inogen Inc stock (US45780L1044): Is its portable oxygen model strong enough for long-term investor r
summary: Inogen Inc specializes in portable oxygen concentrators, targeting the growing home healthcare market, particularly for aging populations. The company's model focuses on lightweight, portable devices for chronic respiratory conditions, leveraging direct-to-consumer sales and insurance reimbursements. While positioned for growth due to demographic shifts and regulatory tailwinds, the article highlights challenges such as reimbursement cuts and competitive pressures that could impact long-term investor returns.

title: JetBlue CEO Says Airline Isn’t Considering Filing for Bankruptcy
summary: JetBlue Airways CEO Joanna Geraghty assured employees that the airline is not considering filing for bankruptcy protection this year. This statement addresses concerns arising from high fuel costs and recent speculation by the carrier's founder about its financial stability. The company aims to quell fears surrounding its future operations.

title: Southern Company boosts quarterly dividend to 76c from 74c
summary: Southern Company (SO) announced an increase in its quarterly dividend from 74 cents to 76 cents per share, payable on June 8 to shareholders of record as of May 18. This marks 25 consecutive years of dividend increases for the company, raising the annualized rate to $3.04 per share. The company emphasizes its commitment to total shareholder return and delivering predictable value.

title: Duke Energy: 100-Year Dividend Track Record — But $103B Capex Is Compressing The Credit Cushion
summary: Duke Energy boasts a 100-year dividend track record and strong operational foundation, but its massive $103 billion capital expenditure plan is putting pressure on its credit metrics. While the dividend is well-covered, the FFO-to-debt ratio is currently below Moody's target, raising concerns about the company's ability to maintain its credit cushion as it spends heavily before new revenues come online. Investors should monitor FFO-to-debt trajectory and the timely conversion of data center agreements into actual load.

title: Southern Company increases dividend for 25th consecutive year
summary: Southern Company has announced a regular quarterly dividend of 76 cents per share, marking the 25th consecutive year of dividend increases. This raises the annualized dividend to $3.04 per share. Chairman, President and CEO Chris Womack emphasized that this achievement underscores the company's commitment to total shareholder return and delivering predictable value.

title: [DEF 14A] INTERNATIONAL BANCSHARES CORP Definitive Proxy Statement
summary: International Bancshares Corporation will hold its 2026 annual shareholder meeting on May 18, 2026, to vote on electing eight directors, ratifying RSM US LLP as auditor, and approving executive compensation. The proxy statement details the company's corporate governance, executive compensation policies, and director independence. Shareholders of record as of April 1, 2026, are entitled to vote on these proposals.

title: Wells Fargo & Company (WFC) Gets Downgraded to Hold by KGI Securities
summary: KGI Securities has downgraded Wells Fargo & Company (NYSE: WFC) to "Hold" from "Outperform," setting an $88 price target. This adjustment followed Wells Fargo's Q1 2026 financial results, which reported net income of $5.3 billion and a diluted EPS of $1.60, with significant increases in diluted EPS, revenue, loans, and deposits compared to the previous year. Despite strong credit performance and capital returns, KGI Securities' downgrade suggests a more cautious outlook on the stock.

title: State Street Had A 'Blockbuster' Q1, Analyst Says
summary: State Street (NYSE:STT) reported a "blockbuster" first quarter, exceeding analyst expectations for both adjusted earnings per share and revenue. CEO Ron O'Hanley highlighted record fee revenue, net interest income, and total revenue, alongside significant operating leverage and pretax margin expansion. Following these strong results, several analysts, including RBC Capital, Truist Securities, Keefe Bruyette & Woods, and Morgan Stanley, increased their price targets for the company.

title: PNC laying off nearly 800 after FirstBank acquisition
summary: PNC Bank is laying off nearly 800 employees following its $4.1 billion acquisition of FirstBank. This move comes less than five months after the acquisition was finalized. The article, written by Lucas High, notes that subscribers can access more details.

title: Magellan joins Rodrigo d’Escoto venture to snag $102M Fulton Market construction loan
summary: A joint venture including Rodrigo d’Escoto of Reflection Window + Wall, Ted Weldon of Weldon Development Group, and now Magellan Development Group, secured a $102 million construction loan from Bank OZK for a multifamily project at 410 North Elizabeth Street in Fulton Market, Chicago. The financing, while significant, is believed to cover only the first phase of the planned 724-unit development, which envisions two towers. This project highlights Bank OZK's continued presence in Fulton Market despite recent turbulence and comes as downtown Chicago faces low apartment vacancy rates and a scarcity of new supply.

title: Potomac Edison Customers in Maryland Can Lower Their Bills With New Residential Time-of-Use Rate
summary: Potomac Edison has introduced a new residential time-of-use rate for its Maryland customers, allowing them to pay less for electricity during off-peak hours. This rate encourages shifting high-energy activities to times when demand on the grid is lower, such as mornings, late evenings, and weekends. Customers can enroll by calling 1-800-686-0011 and will have their meter replaced to track on-peak and off-peak usage.

title: ImmunityBio Inc stock (US45258J1025): Is its immunotherapy pipeline strong enough to unlock new upsi
summary: ImmunityBio Inc. is developing innovative immunotherapies to combat cancer and infectious diseases, focusing on T-cell activation and natural killer cell therapies. Its lead candidate, Anktiva, shows promise in bladder cancer and other solid tumors, positioning the company as a key player in the expanding oncology immunotherapy market. Investors should monitor regulatory milestones, clinical trial data, and potential partnerships while considering the risks associated with biotech investments.

title: Director at Taylor Devices (TAYD) granted 7,000 stock options
summary: Taylor Devices, Inc. director Armenat Fritz Eric was granted 7,000 stock options as compensation on April 18, 2026. The options have an exercise price of $56.43 per share and are set to expire on April 18, 2036. This transaction is a grant/award acquisition, not a market buy or sell, and increases his direct holdings to 7,000 stock options.

title: Taylor Devices (TAYD) director receives 7,000 stock options at 56.43 strike
summary: Taylor Devices (TAYD) director Robert Michael Carey was granted 7,000 stock options with an exercise price of $56.43 per share. These options are exercisable into 7,000 shares of common stock and expire on April 18, 2036. This grant is a compensation-related award, designed to align the director's incentives with the company's long-term performance.

title: Director John Burgess receives 7,000 Taylor Devices (TAYD) stock options at $56.43
summary: Taylor Devices director John Burgess was granted 7,000 stock options with an exercise price of $56.43 per share on April 18, 2026. This compensation grant, reported in a Form 4 filing, means he now holds options for 7,000 shares directly. The options have an expiration date of April 18, 2036.

title: Taylor Devices (TAYD) CFO receives grant of 7,000 stock options at $56.43
summary: Taylor Devices' CFO, Paul Murray Heary, has been granted 7,000 stock options at an exercise price of $56.43 per share, set to expire on April 18, 2036. This equity award is compensation-related and does not involve open-market trading. The transaction was reported in a Form 4 filing, increasing his derivative holdings to 7,000 stock options.

title: Silvercorp Metals Inc stock (CA8672241079): Is its China silver production edge strong enough for gl
summary: Silvercorp Metals Inc. focuses on low-cost silver and gold mining in China, offering investors targeted exposure to silver demand without broad emerging market risks. The company's operational discipline, high-grade Chinese assets, and cost-efficient underground mining model provide a strong competitive edge against global peers. While geopolitical tensions and metal price volatility pose risks, Silvercorp's strategy positions it as a resilient play on precious metals, especially with increasing industrial demand for silver in green energy transitions.

title: TD (TD) issues callable SPY‑linked barrier notes — 7.25% contingent interest, $1,000 principal
summary: The Toronto-Dominion Bank (TD) is offering callable contingent interest barrier notes linked to the State Street SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY). These notes carry a 7.25% annual contingent interest rate, payable semiannually only if SPY's closing value is at or above a 65% barrier. TD can call the notes semiannually, exposing investors to reinvestment risk, and while the estimated value on the pricing date was $981.90, the public offering price was $1,000.
